title: aita for demanding money from the person whose cat i found?
text: for the past week and a half, a stray cat has been hanging around my house. young male (1-2 years old), not fixed, no collar. he’s been getting into fights with the neighbor’s cats so he’s a little roughed up. i asked around the neighborhood, people have seen him but didn’t know where he came from or who owns him. i posted a found poster online, received a few inquiries, but no one claimed him. so, i brought him to the veterinarian, got him sterilized and vaccinated and decided to keep him. the vet said that there was no microchip and he also had a skin condition that needed some medication. literally the day i brought him home from the vet i received a call from a woman who had previously contacted me. she said that she was mistaken before and that it *is* her cat and she wants to come by to pick him up. wibta if i asked for compensation for the vet visit for sterilization and medication before allowing her to take him back? we have a huge problem with unwanted pets in our area (there isn’t even a shelter because there are too many abandoned cats and dogs) and i think it’s irresponsible for a pet owner to not get their pets fixed. i think i might be the asshole because the veterinarian visit did cost a decent amount of money that the old owner didn't agree to pay.

storyboard.content: For the past week and a half, a stray cat has been hanging around my house. He’s a young male, not fixed, no collar, and has been getting into fights with the neighbor’s cats.
I asked around the neighborhood and posted a found poster online. I received a few inquiries, but no one claimed him.
I brought him to the veterinarian, got him sterilized and vaccinated, and decided to keep him. The vet confirmed there was no microchip and found a skin condition that needed medication.
The day I brought the cat home from the vet, a woman who had previously contacted me called back and said she was mistaken before and that it is her cat, and she wants to come pick him up.
I want to ask her for compensation for the vet visit for sterilization and medication before allowing her to take the cat back.

We have a huge problem with unwanted pets in our area — there isn’t even a shelter because there are too many abandoned cats and dogs. I think it’s irresponsible for a pet owner to not get their pets fixed.
The veterinarian visit cost a decent amount of money that the old owner didn’t agree to pay.
